programmes.show_description: How do they turn seaweed into sushi wrapping? How do they make a beer bottle that lasts for fifteen years? How do they keep surfers safe from man-eating sharks?
How do they cast the streetlamps of Venice? How do they prevent another Titanic-like disaster? How do they grow tiny trees for the ancient Chinese art of penjing?
Bond Bug. Who said three-wheeled cars can't be desirable? Mike goes on the hunt for a truly iconic car - the 1970s Bond Bug. It's orange, has no doors and only three wheels.
Land Rover Defender. Mike tries to find a V8-powered Land Rover Defender that Edd can turn into a cool urban cruiser. With just £5,000 can he find a decent car and spec it up?
Trials And Tribulations. At a large house in Devon's countryside, Henry and Sam find piles of cars and bikes everywhere they look. With so much to choose from, what will they bid on?
The Morris Man. Henry and Sam are in East Anglia, checking out a collection of Morris Minors and motorbikes. The duo spot a few bikes they like, including a nice fixer-upper.
Brushes, blackboards, smoked salmon and zips. More items are put under the microscope to discover where they came from.
3D signs, hardwood floors, corrugated pipes. Everyday items are put under the microscope as we discover how they're made.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Dominic is under pressure to restore a faded memorial board. Amanda and Julie work on a cherished teddy bear, while Geoff revamps a one-armed bandit.
Nature In Design. Drew and Alister hunt for pieces inspired by nature, from a folk-art bird's head walking stick in Brighton to a fireside tiger in Shropshire.
Heritage Heroes. Drew and Al buy from heritage sellers, snapping up a curved oak settle made in 1810, and century-old steel tables from a Northampton blacksmith.
The Dirt Dogs' huge dry blower shuts down due to extreme temperatures, while the Mahoneys and Gold Gypsies take a last-minute gamble that delivers big gold.
Neville and Mick's final hunt of the season is set to deliver another monster nugget. And, Scrappers boss, Alex, pays the ultimate price for gambling his life savings.
Paul Martin and Raj Bisram continue their road trip in Wiltshire. Will a gamble buy of antique jelly moulds cause an auction wobble for one expert?
Angus is asked to help with an emotional house clearance in Stockton-on-Tees. Later, he is invited to examine the work of two semi-professional artists.
Dominic is under pressure to restore a faded memorial board. Amanda and Julie work on a cherished teddy bear, while Geoff revamps a one-armed bandit.
Triple Threat. In Oxfordshire, Henry and Sam meet three brothers with containers full of bikes, tractors and other trinkets. They're after a motorbike, but can they find more?
Rust In Peace. Henry and Sam are in Devon, sorting through a workshop full of old signs, petrol pumps and vintage cars. Can they find some gems amongst all the rust?
Rob. Henry and Fuzz visit Rob in Cambridgeshire and find vintage agricultural gems. Can they get some old workhorses running and make a profit?
Angie & Jon. Henry and Fuzz visit Angie and Jon in Bucks and uncover a Francis Barnett and vintage Raleighs. Can they revive them and turn a profit?
Cheryl Baker & Syd Little. Angus helps Eurovision icon Cheryl Baker sell some pop memorabilia. Later he helps comedy legend Syd Little raise funds for his new pet project.
TV presenter Anthea reveals a secret talent to Angus at her dad's home. And he finds a woolly mammoth tusk at Coronation Street star Simon's house.
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit.
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit.
Ross. Henry and Fuzz visit Ross in Hampshire, where they uncover a Nuffield tractor and sheds packed full of tractors, memorabilia, and more hidden gems.
Andrew. Henry and Fuzz visit Andrew in Warwickshire, where sheds overflow with all manner of bikes, cars, and automotive memorabilia.
Marshalling The Raw Recruits. New staff are thrown in the deep end on a rare Lotus, and a unique race car. Richard's beloved steam-car restoration threatens to run out of puff.
1950 Austin Atlantic. Henry and Fuzz head to Bristol to see Robert's 1950 Austin Atlantic. Faded and forgotten for years, can they revive this bold British classic?
How do they make one of the world's fastest bikes? How do pilots train for death-defying acrobatics? And, how do they bring oil and gas up from the bottom of the sea?
How do they hand-roll New York bagels? How do they make a racing car tyre to grip the track at 320 km an hour? And, how do they make lifelike sculptures out of glass?
How do they collect the trash in road-free Venice? How do they make Australia's famous sheepskin boots? And, how do they turn pond algae into a health supplement?
How do they turn rosebuds into Turkish Delight? How do they hammer out a silver spoon? And, how do they make sunscreen to block out harmful UV light?
Volvo P1800s. The team can't wait to get hold of a Volvo P1800 but the problem is, these cars are rare and Mike can only find a single car that's in budget. Will it be good enough?
Subaru Impreza WRX. Mike finds a clean WRX at a good price. Mike decides to make it stand out by making it go faster and look better. Edd has plenty of ideas on how to do both!
Triple Threat. In Oxfordshire, Henry and Sam meet three brothers with containers full of bikes, tractors and other trinkets. They're after a motorbike, but can they find more?
Rust In Peace. Henry and Sam are in Devon, sorting through a workshop full of old signs, petrol pumps and vintage cars. Can they find some gems amongst all the rust?
Ceramic tiles, nuts, steel forgings and skateboards. More everyday items are put under the microscope as we discover how they're made.
Car engines, flour, recliners and envelopes. More everyday items are put under the microscope as we discover how they're made.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Will is tasked with repairing a beloved 3D wooden boat puzzle. Kirsten pieces together a little cat teapot, and Domonic restores a wooden carousel horse.
Viki's New Shop. Drew and Viki buy for Viki's new Edinburgh premises. They find deer antlers from a Cumbrian country house and industrial tables from a former tannery.
Curated Collections. Drew and Al trawl collections curated by experts, unearthing a rare item of motoring history and a 19th-century model ship with a tale to tell.
The Victoria Diggers are back on the hunt for multi-million-dollar nuggets. Plus, rookie team the Dust Devils run into trouble with a new dry blower.
The Poseidon Crew face a devastating financial disaster after permit delays. The Victoria Diggers are still awaiting approval to dig German Gully.
Raj Bisram and Paul Martin explore antique shops in the West Country. At auction a pricey biscuit tin leaves a foul taste while a cheap oil painting makes an impression.
A recently retired woman wants to declutter her home and raise money to visit her family abroad. Angus travels to Horsforth to dig through her collection.
Will is tasked with repairing a beloved 3D wooden boat puzzle. Kirsten pieces together a little cat teapot, and Domonic restores a wooden carousel horse.
Stuck In The Mud. In Worcestershire, Henry and Sam meet a farmer with a shed full of bikes that don't appeal to the guys. However, there's a surprise waiting in a nearby field.
Bike, Bike, Bike...Boat? Henry and Sam are in Yorkshire looking at a collection of Triumph motorcycles that are a bit out of their price range. But is there something else to find?
Ross. Henry and Fuzz visit Ross in Hampshire, where they uncover a Nuffield tractor and sheds packed full of tractors, memorabilia, and more hidden gems.
Andrew. Henry and Fuzz visit Andrew in Warwickshire, where sheds overflow with all manner of bikes, cars, and automotive memorabilia.
Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen & Crissy Rock. Comedian and actor Crissy gets a surprise at auction. Angus has a laugh at Laurence's mansion, where he finds a big porcelain pumpkin.
Frazer Hines & Jenny Powell. Angus visits an ex-Emmerdale actor and a TV presenting legend. They then come to his auction house to see items from a Tardis to a boyband bed!
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit.
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit.
Henry. The guys visit Hampshire, where a shed full of motorbikes leads them to a rare Yamaha Maxam, but getting it going again proves trickier than expected.
Duncan. Henry and Fuzz visit Duncan in Wiltshire, where they uncover a mighty vintage diesel road roller hidden amongst sheds packed with farm machinery.
Janet Street-Porter & Louis Smith. Auctioneer Angus helps Louis Smith sell Olympic gear and movie memorabilia, and clears Janet Street-Porter's old windmill of unwanted clutter.
Toys, Toys, Teddys and Dolls. Angus takes a trip down memory lane as two cherished childhood collections go under his gavel. For one customer, it's a very emotional time.
How do they create the world's finest canvases? How do they cook up foul-smelling stinky tofu? And, how do they get fifty drinks out of a single bottle of beer?
How do they look after bees to make millions of jars of honey? How do they build a trike to carry the whole family, and how to stop a bullet with a sheet of ceramic?
